questions.q: In which Indian city is the Indian Institute of Science (IISc) located?
questions.explanation: IISc is located in Bengaluru (Bangalore), Karnataka.

questions.q: Which Indian industrialist's vision led to the founding of IISc?
questions.explanation: IISc was founded through the vision and philanthropy of Jamsetji Tata, which is why it is also called the Tata Institute.

questions.q: In which year was the Indian Institute of Science established?
questions.explanation: IISc was established in 1909 in Bengaluru.

questions.q: IISc is widely regarded as India's premier institute for which area of work?
questions.explanation: IISc is India's foremost institute for advanced scientific and technological research.

questions.q: By what nickname is IISc commonly known among students and locals?
questions.explanation: Because of its origins in Jamsetji Tata's endowment, IISc is popularly called the Tata Institute.

questions.q: Which famous physicist who discovered a light-scattering effect served as Director of IISc?
questions.explanation: Nobel laureate C. V. Raman, discoverer of the Raman effect, was the first Indian Director of IISc.

questions.q: What type of institution is IISc primarily?
questions.explanation: IISc is a deemed university focused on graduate education and research in science and engineering.

questions.q: The royal family of which princely state donated land for IISc's campus?
questions.explanation: The Maharaja of Mysore donated land in Bengaluru that became the IISc campus.

questions.q: IISc primarily offers programs at which level?
questions.explanation: IISc has historically focused on master's, doctoral, and advanced research programs, later adding an undergraduate science program.

questions.q: In which Indian state is IISc situated?
questions.explanation: Bengaluru, where IISc is located, is the capital of Karnataka.

questions.q: Who was the first Director of the Indian Institute of Science when it opened?
questions.explanation: British chemist Morris W. Travers served as the first Director of IISc from 1909.

questions.q: C. V. Raman became the first Indian Director of IISc in which decade?
questions.explanation: C. V. Raman was appointed the first Indian Director of IISc in 1933.

questions.q: Which aerospace pioneer, later a key figure in India's space program, was associated with IISc and founded its aeronautics activity?
questions.explanation: Satish Dhawan, who later led ISRO, was a long-serving Director of IISc and built up its aerospace engineering work.

questions.q: Apart from a government grant, IISc's founding was supported by an endowment from which source?
questions.explanation: The Tata family's endowment, set up from Jamsetji Tata's bequest, was central to founding IISc.

questions.q: Which historic landmark building stands at the centre of the IISc main campus?
options: The Main Building with its central tower and dome
questions.explanation: The IISc Main Building, with its distinctive tower, is the iconic centrepiece of the campus.

questions.q: C. V. Raman won the Nobel Prize in Physics for his work, which he was continuing around the time he joined IISc. In which year did he win it?
questions.explanation: C. V. Raman won the 1930 Nobel Prize in Physics for the discovery of the Raman effect.

questions.q: IISc holds the status of which kind of academic institution under Indian law?
options: A research wing of a single company
questions.explanation: IISc is a deemed-to-be university, giving it autonomy to award its own degrees.

questions.q: Which of these fields is a major research strength at IISc?
questions.explanation: Aerospace engineering is one of IISc's flagship departments, with deep links to India's space and aviation programs.

questions.q: IISc is frequently ranked as the top institute in India in which category of higher-education rankings?
questions.explanation: IISc regularly tops national rankings such as NIRF in the university and research-institution categories.

questions.q: Which engineer-statesman is historically credited with helping plan and locate IISc on the Bengaluru site?
questions.explanation: The engineer and Diwan of Mysore M. Visvesvaraya was closely involved in IISc's early development; Lord Curzon as Viceroy also supported the founding.

questions.q: An undergraduate program at IISc, introduced in the 21st century, primarily awards which degree?
questions.explanation: IISc launched a four-year Bachelor of Science (Research) program in 2011 to admit students after school.

questions.q: The early teaching at IISc began with departments in which two foundational subjects?
options: General and Applied Chemistry, and Electrical Technology
questions.explanation: IISc opened with departments in general and applied chemistry and in electrical technology.

questions.q: The discovery of the Raman effect, by IISc's famous director, falls in which branch of physics?
questions.explanation: The Raman effect concerns the inelastic scattering of light, a foundational result in spectroscopy.

questions.q: Satish Dhawan, an IISc Director, later headed ISRO. The Indian launch site at Sriharikota is named in his honour as the:
questions.explanation: Sriharikota's launch complex is named the Satish Dhawan Space Centre after the former IISc Director and ISRO chairman.

questions.q: IISc has played a major role in establishing which national high-performance computing or supercomputing-related initiatives in India?
options: Centre for high-performance and supercomputing research
questions.explanation: IISc hosts leading supercomputing and high-performance computing facilities supporting national research.

questions.q: The viceroy whose support during his tenure helped formalise the founding scheme of IISc in the 1900s was:
questions.explanation: Lord Curzon's administration was instrumental in approving the scheme that established IISc.

questions.q: Which renowned solid-state and materials chemist served as Director of IISc and was awarded the Bharat Ratna?
questions.explanation: C. N. R. Rao, a leading solid-state and materials chemist, was Director of IISc from 1984 to 1994 and received the Bharat Ratna in 2013.

questions.q: IISc's department long known for materials work is historically called the Department of:
questions.explanation: IISc's metallurgy department, now materials engineering, is among its oldest and most distinguished.

questions.q: Which biotechnology and biological-sciences strength is IISc particularly noted for?
questions.explanation: IISc is renowned for molecular biophysics, biochemistry, and the biological sciences.

questions.q: IISc's founding required cooperation among three principal parties. Which set best describes them?
options: The Tatas, the Government of India, and the Maharaja of Mysore
The United Nations, NASA, and Oxford
questions.explanation: IISc was founded through the partnership of the Tata endowment, the Government of India, and the Maharaja of Mysore who provided land.
